channel.title: Cross Country Evangelism Newsletter
channel.description: Quarterly updates from Cross Country Evangelism.

item.title: Jamaica 2026 Newsletter
item.description: January 6-27, 2026: updates from Cross Country Evangelism's sixteenth Jamaica outreach, with prayer requests and ministry highlights.
item.content:encoded:
View this newsletter on the web.

Share: Facebook X Email

Jamaica 2026 Newsletter

January 6-27, 2026

Sixteenth Missions Trip to Jamaica

Cross Country Evangelism just wrapped up its 16th missions trip to the island of Jamaica, the Caribbean jewel, from January 6-27, 2026. The outreach took place with six evangelist preachers from New Hampshire, New York, Virginia, Missouri, and the United Kingdom laboring together. This mission began in 2009 when Mike Stockwell and Robert Gray met, decided to join efforts during the New England winter months, and looked for a place where they could continue proclaiming the good news of Jesus Christ. Their first Jamaica trip followed in January 2010, when they spent a month declaring biblical truth twice a day in Montego Bay. Over the years, believers from across the United States and other countries have joined them, with as many as 24 Christians laboring in a single year to share the gospel with the people of Jamaica.

Why Jamaica?

Jamaica is a vibrant, culturally rich, and beautiful country filled with warm and helpful people, yet it is also steeped in many religions and cultic influences. The island, with a population of about 2.83 million, needs the truth of the gospel preached in its streets just like every other nation in the world. This year's team labored in Montego Bay, Falmouth, Ocho Rios, Negril, and Lucea. Along the way they spoke with people from every walk of life: young and old, men and women, boys and girls, religious people, pagans, professing Christians, Seventh Day Adventists, Oneness Pentecostals, Rastafarians, Yeshua Only adherents, Hebrew Israelites, Jehovah's Witnesses, Mormons, and others.

Openness to Conversation and Literature

Even though the island is filled with religious groups and false religion, Cross Country Evangelism has not found a place more open to conversations about the person and work of the Lord Jesus Christ or to the distribution of Christian books and gospel tracts. The literature is usually received well, some people ask for extra copies to share, and in some cases they read it immediately and begin asking questions. Beyond questions about the literature itself, many Jamaicans ask biblical questions and some show real concern for their souls.

Daily Preaching and Providence on the Road

The team prayed before and during the trip that God would grant tender hearts, especially in the aftermath of Hurricane Melissa, which made landfall on October 28, 2025. Despite storm damage, many people appeared to have quickly returned to normal life. Still, every day the gospel of Jesus Christ was heralded in public squares throughout the island with the call to repent and believe in the only true Savior of the world. One of the most memorable days of the trip came during the longest drive. After striking a pothole, the team suffered a blown tire and had many gospel conversations with those who stopped to help. After the tire was repaired, they continued on and experienced a tremendous afternoon of preaching and witnessing. On the return trip they had a second blown tire, which opened up still more conversations about what it means to be a Christian and to be saved by the Lord Jesus Christ.

Fruit, Prayer, and Looking Ahead

During the outreach, the one-to-one conversations were too many to count. Tens of thousands heard the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God. People asked for prayer, some expressed a desire to be saved, and the saints were greatly encouraged. All of the literature was given away, including more than 17,000 tracts and 115 Bibles. Please join Cross Country Evangelism in praying that the Holy Spirit would move upon the hearts of the Jamaican people, bring salvation, reap a great harvest across the island, and grant the team another opportunity to return in January 2027.

2 Corinthians 4:1-7

Therefore, having this ministry by the mercy of God, we do not lose heart. But we have renounced disgraceful, underhanded ways. We refuse to practice cunning or to tamper with God's word, but by the open statement of the truth we would commend ourselves to everyone's conscience in the sight of God. And even if our gospel is veiled, it is veiled to those who are perishing. In their case the god of this world has blinded the minds of the unbelievers, to keep them from seeing the light of the gospel of the glory of Christ, who is the image of God. For what we proclaim is not ourselves, but Jesus Christ as Lord, with ourselves as your servants for Jesus' sake. For God, who said, "Let light shine out of darkness," has shone in our hearts to give the light of the knowledge of the glory of God in the face of Jesus Christ. But we have this treasure in jars of clay, to show that the surpassing power belongs to God and not to us.

item.title: Fall 2025 Newsletter
item.description: September-December 2025: ministry updates and photo highlights from NYC, Massachusetts, Ohio, and Virginia.
item.content:encoded:
View this newsletter on the web.

Share: Facebook X Email

Fall 2025 Newsletter

September-December 2025

Thank you for praying for Cross Country Evangelism. Here are the latest ministry updates and photo highlights from our fall travels.

New York City Outreach

It has been a great few months for Cross Country Evangelism. We have traveled to the Northeast, the Midwest, and the Southeast with the gospel. We participated with ATL's Voice in NYC for the week of September 11th. Jason Cantrell has been inviting evangelists to open air preach with him around the city for the week culminating with a gospel outreach at Ground Zero. We heralded the truth and interacted with many people throughout the city, on the subway, the Staten Island Ferry, Battery Park, Time Square, Union Square, and Washington Square Park. We even spent a day preaching at the infamous Planned Parenthood in Manhattan on Bleeker Street which is now permanently closed. Going to the Big Apple gives the opportunity to share the gospel with people from all over the world who are always willing to stop and dialogue.

Massachusetts Campus Outreach

The last week of October we teamed with some saints in Massachusetts to labor at a few college campuses; Salem State University, the University of Connecticut, and UMass Lowell. Overall, the students at the schools in Massachusetts were quite beside themselves, yet it is to be expected at those two institutions. At Salem State we were greeted with a rough LGBTQ reception with some of the students spouting tolerance yet showing their intolerance towards the gospel message. The students at UMass Lowell provided a very similar reception with one student throwing milk on one of the preachers and was quickly arrested for disorderly behavior and another student stole a sign and was apprehended by the police shortly afterwards as well. There was even an article in the school paper about the outreach that day. Despite the foolishness and folly, the Good News still went forward to the many students and facility at the institutions for the glory of King Jesus. By God's grace the Christian students on those campuses were very encouraged we came to take a stand for Christ, and some were compelled to share the gospel with their classmates while we were there and they engaged us with questions about being better at evangelism, they received and provided prayer and shared their testimonies. We finished our time in the state with an outreach on the streets of Salem, MA on Halloween. People come from all over the world for the holiday celebration and it affords a great opportunity to proclaim the gospel to tens of thousands of people, young and old in the center of the city well into the evening.

Ohio Ministry Stops

We visited with the saints in Ohio at LaRue Baptist Church, Providence Church, and Redeemer Covenant Church. We were able to do ministry at Cleveland State University with pastors Austin Hetsler and Ralph Hull and gave away many gospel tracts and preached the gospel to throngs of people in the thousands and gave away hundreds of tracts before the Ohio State University football game against the team from Rutgers with our dear brother Michael Coughlin.

Virginia in December

The first weekend in December we traveled to Hampton, VA to meet with Don "Stonewall" Karns and present the Good News at the Dominion Energy Christmas Parade in Richmond, VA. It is a great venue; the people are attentive and mostly take the tracts and some even applaud when the preaching is done. Later in the afternoon we took the gospel to Colonial Williamsburg where the street and sidewalks are filled with many families and shoppers who come for the Grand Illumination Fireworks. It is a great opportunity to give witness of the Lord Jesus Christ during the Christmas Season.

Prayer and Travel Notes

In Salem, Mike has had an ongoing interaction over the years with a transvestite whom he has been trying to win to Christ. He seems like hard ground, however Mike continues to pray for his salvation. This year in direct answer to prayer the Lord enabled a little bit longer of a conversation between them than the two have had in the past. In October Robert traveled to London with Sports Fans Outreach International for gospel preaching throughout the city of London during the week and for two NFL football games at massive venues. He also labored with Phil Close and Jimmy Hamilton in their respective "fishing holes" in the Birmingham and Nottingham area in the UK. In November Mike attended the Men's Summit Meeting with Christ The Rock Church in Ohio and was edified by the messages and fellowship that went on there.

Next, we travel to Jamacia with a small team for our sixteenth trip to the island. Please keep us in prayer as we go the herald the glorious gospel of Jesus Christ!

Photo Highlights
